 {"id":"caam-by4","title":"Terminal UI (TUI) with Bubble Tea","description":"## Overview
A visual terminal interface for managing caam profiles. While the CLI is fully functional, a TUI provides:
- At-a-glance view of all profiles across providers
- Visual feedback on status (logged in, locked, active)
- Discoverable interface for new users
- Running session management

## Design (from original spec)
Layout:
- Left panel: Provider list (Codex / Claude / Gemini)
- Center panel: Profiles table (Name, Auth mode, Logged-in?, Last used, Notes)
- Right panel: Action pane + detail view for selected profile

Keybindings:
- ↑/↓: Select profile
- Enter: Run default command (launch CLI)
- l: Login/refresh auth
- o: Open account page in browser
- e: Edit profile (notes, browser config)
- d: Delete profile (with confirmation)
- /: Search/filter profiles
- Tab: Switch between providers
- q: Quit

## Implementation
Use Bubble Tea (bubbletea) with:
- bubbles for standard components (list, table, textinput)
- lipgloss for styling

## User Value
- Visual overview prevents mistakes (wrong account)
- Running sessions visible prevents data corruption
- Lower learning curve than CLI-only

## Consideration
TUI should be the default when running 'caam' with no args, but all functionality must remain available via CLI for scripting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:51:16.807075968-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:46:22.843793337-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:46:22.843793337-05:00","close_reason":"All TUI components implemented: scaffolding (caam-qpx), provider panel (caam-ta7), profiles table (caam-nmm), detail panel (caam-f9h), keyboard navigation (caam-j14). TUI is fully functional with three-panel layout and all keybindings wired up."}
 {"id":"caam-cfb","title":"Usage analytics CLI command","description":"## Purpose
Add `caam usage` command to display profile usage statistics from the command line.

## Background
Users should be able to see their usage patterns to understand which profiles
they use most, identify underutilized accounts, and optimize their workflow.

## Command Structure
```bash
# Summary view (default)
caam usage

# Detailed view for specific profile
caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed

# Time range filtering
caam usage --days 30
caam usage --since 2025-12-01

# Output formats
caam usage --json
caam usage --csv
```

## Example Output
```
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs
gemini/default       3 sessions    0.5 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed
Session History for claude/work
───────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
...

Total: 42 sessions, 18.5 hours over 7 days
Average session: 26 minutes
Most active day: Monday (12 sessions)
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// cmd/caam/usage.go
var usageCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "usage",
    Short: "Display profile usage statistics",
    RunE:  runUsage,
}

func init() {
    usageCmd.Flags().StringP("profile", "p", "", "Profile to show (provider/name)")
    usageCmd.Flags().Bool("detailed", false, "Show detailed session history")
    usageCmd.Flags().Int("days", 7, "Number of days to include")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("format", "table", "Output format: table, json, csv")
}

func runUsage(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    db, err := db.Open()
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer db.Close()
    
    // Query and format based on flags
}
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/caam/usage.go
- cmd/caam/usage_test.go
- Modify cmd/caam/root.go to add subcommand

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary shows all profiles with session count and duration
- [ ] Detailed view shows individual sessions for a profile
- [ ] Time range filtering works correctly
- [ ] JSON and CSV export work
- [ ] Handles empty database gracefully
- [ ] Performance acceptable for large activity logs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:53.646488393-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:11:12.338404628-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:11:12.338404628-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam usage command with summary/detailed views and json/csv output.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-cfb","depends_on_id":"caam-r36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:28:57.057526979-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting caam usage command: implement cmd/caam/cmd/usage.go with summary + detailed profile view, since/days filtering, and table/json/csv output using internal/db.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:06:53Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented  command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:10:45Z"},{"id":23,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"(Follow-up) Implemented the caam usage command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:11:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-ckk","title":"E2E: CLI command workflow tests","description":"End-to-end integration tests for CLI command workflows:

## Test Scenarios
1. `caam list` - List all profiles across providers
2. `caam backup <provider> <email>` - Backup current auth state
3. `caam restore <provider> <email>` - Restore saved profile
4. `caam delete <provider> <email>` - Delete a saved profile
5. `caam status` - Show current auth status for all providers

## Requirements
- Use t.TempDir() for isolated file system tests
- Real file operations (no mocks)
- Test actual cobra command execution
- Detailed logging at each step
- Test error cases (missing files, invalid profiles)

## Logging Format
Each test should log:
- [START] Test name and description
- [SETUP] Temp directory and fixture creation
- [EXEC] Command being executed
- [VERIFY] Expected vs actual results
- [CLEANUP] Resources cleaned up
- [PASS/FAIL] Final result with duration","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:52:40.357727069-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T02:15:21.938011493-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T02:15:21.938011493-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 9 E2E tests for CLI command workflows: list, backup, restore, delete, status, clear, profile workflow, invalid tool error handling, multi-provider isolation.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ckk","depends_on_id":"caam-7a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:54:53.822477208-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-ckk","depends_on_id":"caam-aek","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:55:26.082995053-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-dmw","title":"Project management CLI commands","description":"## Purpose
Add CLI commands for managing project-profile associations.

## Commands

### caam project set
Associate current directory with a profile.
```bash
caam project set claude client-a@work.com
# Output: Associated /home/user/work/client-a with claude/client-a@work.com
```

### caam project list
Show all project associations.
```bash
caam project list
# Output:
# /home/user/work/client-a
#   claude: client-a@work.com
#   codex:  work-main
#
# /home/user/personal/blog
#   claude: personal@gmail.com
```

### caam project show
Show associations for current directory.
```bash
cd /home/user/work/client-a
caam project show
# Output:
# Project: /home/user/work/client-a
# Associations:
#   claude: client-a@work.com
#   codex:  work-main
```

### caam project remove
Remove association.
```bash
caam project remove claude
# Output: Removed claude association from /home/user/work/client-a
```

### caam project clear
Remove all associations for current directory.
```bash
caam project clear
# Output: Cleared all associations from /home/user/work/client-a
```

## Auto-activation Integration
When running `caam activate claude` without specifying a profile:
1. Check if CWD has an association
2. If yes, use that profile automatically
3. If no, use default or prompt user

```bash
cd /home/user/work/client-a
caam activate claude
# Output: Using project association: claude/client-a@work.com
# Activated.
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/caam/project.go (project subcommand)
- cmd/caam/project_test.go
- cmd/caam/activate.go (add project check)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] project set associates current directory
- [ ] project list shows all associations
- [ ] project show displays current directory associations
- [ ] project remove deletes single provider association
- [ ] project clear removes all associations
- [ ] activate uses project association when available
- [ ] Clear error messages for edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:25:49.003391908-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:56:39.484397921-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:56:39.484397921-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented `caam project` commands (set/list/show/remove/clear) backed by internal/project store, added project-aware `caam activate <tool>` (optional profile) using CWD association or default profile, and added tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-dmw","depends_on_id":"caam-ir7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:35.619607689-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"caam-dmw","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting implementation of `caam project` commands (set/list/show/remove/clear) and project-aware `caam activate <tool>` that uses CWD association when present (and config allows). Will add tests + wire into root command.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:48:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-dw4","title":"Implement 'open' command to open account pages","description":"## Task
Add 'caam open' command to open the provider's account management page in the browser.

## Usage
```bash
caam open codex          # Open OpenAI account page
caam open claude work-1  # Open Anthropic account page (using work-1's browser config)
caam open gemini         # Open Google account page
```

## Account URLs
- Codex/OpenAI: https://platform.openai.com/account
- Claude/Anthropic: https://console.anthropic.com/
- Gemini/Google: https://myaccount.google.com/ or https://aistudio.google.com/

## Browser Profile Integration
If a profile is specified and has browser config, open URL in that browser profile. This ensures you see the correct account's dashboard.

## Implementation
- Define account URLs per provider
- Use browser launcher (from browser epic) to open URL
- If no profile specified, use default browser

## Dependencies
- Should use browser launcher from browser epic
- Can work without it (fall back to default browser)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Opens correct URL for each provider
- Uses browser profile if configured
- Falls back to default browser if no config","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:55:03.513290492-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:39:14.018599226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:39:14.018599226-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented caam open command to open provider account pages with browser profile support","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-dw4","depends_on_id":"caam-1ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:55:03.533449267-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-dw4","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:55:03.534802505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-e36","title":"Proactive Token Refresh","description":"# Proactive Token Refresh

## Purpose
Automatically refresh OAuth tokens before they expire, preventing auth failures that disrupt user workflow.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool refreshes tokens 5 minutes before expiry:
```go
if a.ExpiresAt.Before(now.Add(5*time.Minute)) {
    return true  // needs refresh
}
```

## Why This Matters
When tokens expire mid-session:
1. CLI tool fails with auth error
2. User loses context/momentum
3. User must manually re-authenticate
4. Frustrating experience

With proactive refresh:
1. Token refreshed before expiry
2. No disruption to workflow
3. User may not even notice

## What This Epic Delivers
1. Provider-specific token expiry parsing
2. OAuth refresh handlers for Claude, Codex, Gemini
3. Refresh-on-activate logic
4. CLI feedback ("Refreshing token...")
5. Integration with health status

## Refresh Strategy
1. **On profile activation**: If token expires within 10 minutes, refresh
2. **Optional daemon mode**: Background process watches all profiles (future)
3. **Manual trigger**: `caam refresh <provider> <profile>`

## Provider-Specific Details

### Claude Code
- Auth file: `~/.claude.ai/oauth.json` (or similar)
- Expiry: Stored in oauth.json
- Refresh endpoint: TBD (needs investigation)

### OpenAI Codex
- Auth file: `~/.codex/auth.json`
- Token endpoint: `https://auth.openai.com/oauth/token`
- Request format:
```json
{
  "client_id": "app_EMoamEEZ73f0CkXaXp7hrann",
  "grant_type": "refresh_token",
  "refresh_token": "..."
}
```

### Google Gemini
- Auth file: `~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json`
- Token endpoint: `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`
- Request format: Form-encoded (not JSON!)
```
client_id=...&grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token=...
```

## Safety Considerations
1. **Never delete working tokens** - refresh to temp file first
2. **Graceful failure** - if refresh fails, keep old token
3. **Rate limiting** - cooldown between refresh attempts
4. **Offline handling** - warn but do not fail
5. **Atomic writes** - use temp file + rename pattern

## User Experience
```bash
$ caam activate claude work
Refreshing token (expires in 4 minutes)... done
Activated profile "work" for claude

$ caam status
claude: work (token valid for 59 minutes) 🟢
codex:  personal (token expired) 🔴
```

## Dependencies
- Profile Health Foundation (for expiry storage)

## Blocks
- Nothing directly (standalone feature)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:16:24.53763408-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:43:58.51948346-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:43:58.51948346-05:00","close_reason":"Fully implemented: refresh package (claude.go, codex.go, gemini.go), refresh CLI command with --all/--dry-run/--force/--quiet flags, refresh-on-activate in activate.go. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-e36","depends_on_id":"caam-3am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:26:43.447146158-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-e36","depends_on_id":"caam-x0g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:03.879152906-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
